parsed_output: Given the provided dataset, we can process the query as follows:
Salmon: type = fish, domestic = False, size = medium
Crocodile: type = reptile, domestic = False, size = large
Eagle: type = bird, domestic = False, size = medium
Hamster: type = mammal, domestic = True, size = small
Goldfish: type = fish, domestic = True, size = small
Turtle: type = reptile, domestic = True, size = small
Snake: type = reptile, domestic = False, size = medium
Lion: type = mammal, domestic = False, size = large
Parrot: type = bird, domestic = True, size = small
Whale: type = mammal, domestic = False, size = large
Breaking down the query:
(x.size ¤ {medium})` means select items where the size is medium.
(x.domestic ¤ {False})` means select items where the domestic status is False.
↯` is the logical OR operator.
[x.name]` means select the name of the item.
Therefore, the query is selecting items that are medium in size and not domestic.
Based on the dataset, the items that match the query are:
Salmon
Crocodile
Eagle
Snake
Whale
So, the output in JSON format is:
["Salmon", "Crocodile", "Eagle", "Snake", "Whale"]
